_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Senning and Mustard Operation/ : Senning and mustard operation_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Senning and mustard operation_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists).
procedure_name: Mustard Operation (Surgical Correction of Category III Congenital Heart Disease)
clinical_indications: TGA with intact ventricular septum presenting beyond 3–4 weeks of life with regressed left ventricle, where Arterial Switch Operation (ASO) is not feasible, for patients presenting between 2 and 6 months of age (Class IIa).
TGA with intact ventricular septum presenting beyond 3–4 weeks of life with regressed left ventricle, for patients presenting between 6 months and 2 years of age (Class IIa).

clinical_thresholds.note: if Arterial Switch Operation (ASO) not feasible

claim.label: Post procedure stills of ECHO with report

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detail history, signs & symptoms, and indication for procedure?
Was the Echo/ Doppler report suggestive of Transposition of Great Arteries?

cpd_claim.q: Are the indoor case papers submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Does the Post procedure still of ECHO show repair of the defect?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?
Is the barcode of implant used submitted?

common_queries: Echo/Doppler report not suggestive of Transposition of Great Arteries at pre-authorization
Clinical notes missing or lacking detailed history, signs, symptoms, or procedure indication at pre-authorization
Post-procedure ECHO stills do not demonstrate repair of the cardiac defect
Barcode of implant not submitted when an implant was used
Mandatory documents for claim submission (Indoor case papers, Procedure/Operative notes, Detailed Discharge Summary) are missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: Transposition of great arteries (TGA) is the most common cyanotic CHD at birth, accounting for approximately 5% of all CHDs. Its prevalence is 2–3/10,000 live births. Boys are affected two to three times more as compared to girls. This anomaly is reported to be more common in infants born to mothers with diabetes, poor nutrition, or history of alcohol intake. In 70% of cases, there is no associated defect apart from ASD, PDA, or insignificant VSD; these cases are labeled as having simple TGA. Association of TGA with other defects such as large VSD and left ventricular outflow tract obstruction or coarctation is referred to as complex TGA. It is a serious disease and most patients with TGA present very early in life, within few days or weeks after birth. With the advent of improved surgical techniques and postoperative care, long-term survival is > 90% with very low reintervention rates.

Clinical assessment: In simple TGA, cyanosis is the dominant feature; no heart murmur is audible in most cases. Those with large VSD and/or PDA present with features of heart failure. Weak femoral pulses indicate associated CoA.
surgical_options: Senning and Mustard operations are type of atrial switch surgeries offered to patients with TGA where arterial switch operation is not feasible.
In the Mustard procedure, the atrial septum is excised, and the atrial baffle is created by the placement of a single elephant trunk-shaped patch made of pericardial tissue or prosthetic patch.
